text:
Home>International>Algerian Boxer Imane Khelif Challenges World Boxing Gender Testing at CAS

Algerian boxer Imane Khelif, who won gold in the under-66 kg category at the Paris 2024 Olympics, has taken legal action against World Boxing’s new gender testing regulations.

Rabat – Algerian boxer Imane Khelif, who won gold in the under-66 kg category at the Paris 2024 Olympics, has taken legal action against World Boxing’s new gender testing regulations.

The Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS)announcedon Monday that it received her appeal, though it stressed that her chances of immediate relief remain slim.

At the center of the dispute is World Boxing’s requirement that female athletes undergo chromosomal testing before competing. Khelif rejects this rule, arguing it unfairly prevents her from taking part in international tournaments.

The regulation already forced her to miss the Eindhoven competition in June, and she now asks to be allowed to enter the upcoming World Championships in Liverpool without the test.

CAS, however, has declined to suspend the decision while the case unfolds.

Khelif’s name firstbecamea flashpoint in Paris, where her victory drew a wave of hostility and disinformation. Alongside Taiwanese boxer Lin Yu-ting, she was targeted by accusations questioning her gender.

The International Olympic Committee had nevertheless stood by the two athletes, confirming their eligibility and affirming that they are women.

The case now brings back an old debate. From 1968 to 1996, Olympic organizers relied on genetic tests to confirm eligibility in women’s categories.

World Boxing has revived that approach, using a PCR method to detect the presence of the SRY gene on the Y chromosome.

Proponents of this test claim it offers a simple solution. Critics, however,argueit unfairly excludes transgender and some intersex athletes.

Khelif’s appeal may not alter her immediate future in Liverpool, but it sets the stage for a legal battle that could influence how sport handles questions of gender for years to come.Tags:Algerian boxerCASImane Khelifkhelifworld boxing organization
